# Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct

## Our Pledge

As members of the **Kntro-Soft** team, we pledge to make participation in this project a harassment-free experience for everyone, regardless of level of experience, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, personal appearance, ethnicity, or religion.

## Our Standards

Examples of behavior that contributes to a positive environment:

- Using inclusive and respectful language
- Being receptive to constructive criticism about the report
- Accepting responsibility for mistakes and learning from them
- Prioritizing what is best for the team and the academic project
- Showing empathy toward other team members

Examples of unacceptable behavior:

- Dismissive comments about another member's work
- Taking ownership of report sections without team consensus
- Ignoring Pull Request reviews without justification
- Merging to `main` without team approval
- Any conduct that could be considered inappropriate in an academic setting

## Our Responsibilities

Each team member is responsible for:

- Keeping `CHANGELOG.md` updated when modifying the report
- Respecting the section assignments defined in `CODEOWNERS`
- Responding to PR reviews within a maximum of **48 hours**
- Communicating blockers or impediments to the team in advance

## Scope

This Code of Conduct applies within the GitHub repository (`Kntro-Soft/Report`) and in any team communication channel related to the project.

## Enforcement

Instances of unacceptable behavior may be reported to the team lead:
**Gutiérrez Soto, Jhosepmyr Orlando** — `jhosepmyrgutierrezsoto@gmail.com`

All complaints will be reviewed and investigated and will result in a response deemed necessary and appropriate to the circumstances.

## Attribution

This Code of Conduct is adapted from the [Contributor Covenant](https://www.contributor-covenant.org), version 2.1.
